description: Proprietary binary format produced by the GlobalSat DG-388 GPS data logger. Each file is a bare sequence of fixed 28-byte records holding one GPS fix plus a record-type flag (automatic log vs. manual waypoint).

fields.name: altitude_m
fields.notes: decimeters above MSL in the binary

fields.name: speed_kmh
fields.notes: hundredths of km/h in the binary

fields.name: record_flag
fields.notes: 2 = automatic log, 3 = manual waypoint (1-click record button)

key_details: Time is UTC, confirmed against GPX exports which suffix `Z`.
Altitude precedes speed in the binary even though the GlobalSat CSV puts speed first.
Bytes 24-27 are two packed uint16s, not a single uint32.
Coordinates use 1e-7 degree precision (~1.1 cm), same convention as FIT files.

validation.notes: Found via csv_correlate.py against the GlobalSat PC tool CSV export at 100% match.

provenance: The format was decoded by running csv_correlate.py against a known-good CSV export from the GlobalSat PC tool, then confirming the remaining heading and record_flag fields with field_probe.py (heading against calculated bearings between consecutive GPS points; record_flag against the single flag=3 record corresponding to a manual waypoint button press while the aircraft was stationary).
